data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Here's my analysis of all legacy patterns found:

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Replacement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import | Implicit via SDK |
| 4 | `TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2 | `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 5 | `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | All SDK defaults — remove |
| 6 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup`s (OutputPath, DebugType, Optimize, etc.) | All SDK defaults — remove |
| 7 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Auto-globbed by SDK |
| 8 | Framework `<Reference>`s (System, System.Core, etc.) | Implicit for `net472` in SDK-style |
| 9 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Move metadata to `<PropertyGroup>`; auto-generated by SDK |
| 10 | `OutputType` Library, `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` matching project name | All defaults — remove |
| 11 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Default for library projects — remove |

Let me apply the modernization now.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>1.0.0.0</FileVersion>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize this project.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` declaration** with `ToolsVersion` and XML namespace — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import** — handled automatically by SDK
3. **Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import** — handled automatically by SDK
4. **Boilerplate PropertyGroups** — `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` are all defaults in SDK-style


5. **Debug/Release configurations** — `DebugSymbols`, `DebugType`, `Optimize`, `OutputPath`, `DefineConstants`, `ErrorReport`, `WarningLevel` are SDK defaults
6. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** → needs to become `TargetFramework` with value `net472`
7. **Explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK-style auto-globs `.cs` files, so these are redundant
8. **Framework `<Reference>` items** — `System`, `System.Core`, `System.Data`, `System.Xml`, `System.Xml.Linq` are implicit in the framework and don't need explicit declaration
9. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style auto-generates assembly attributes, so I can move the metadata into the `.csproj` and delete the file
10. **`OutputType` of `Library`** — this is the default, so it can be removed
11. **`RootNamespace` and `AssemblyName`** — these default to the project name in SDK-style

The modernized `.csproj` will be much leaner. I'm going to consolidate the AssemblyInfo metadata by moving it into the project file and removing the separate file. For the assembly attributes, `AssemblyTitle` becomes the default from `AssemblyName`, `AssemblyDescription` maps to `<Description>`, and `AssemblyCompany` maps to `<Company>`.

Applying the changes now...

data.content:
**Done.** The project went from 47 lines down to 12. Here's a summary of what changed:

**Removed (all SDK defaults):**
- XML declaration, `ToolsVersion`, MSBuild namespace
- `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports
- `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`
- `OutputType` (Library is default), `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` (match project name)
- Entire Debug/Release `PropertyGroup`s (OutputPath, Optimize, DebugType, etc.)
- All `<Compile>` items (auto-globbed by SDK)
- All framework `<Reference>`s (implicit for `net472`)
- `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (SDK auto-generates the assembly attributes)
